Title: A Lover's Discourse: Fragments
description: A philosophical exploration of a lover's inner monologue, broken down into 80 fragments or 'figures' defined as 'gestures of the lover at work.' Barthes uses structuralism and semiology to dissect the language of love, blending literary references from Plato, Freud, Goethe, and Proust with introspective reflections on desire, heartbreak, jealousy, and the limitations of language in expressing love. The language we use when we are in love is not a language we speak. It is a language addressed to ourselves and to our imaginary beloved. It is a language of solitude, of mythology, of what Barthes calls an 'image repertoire'.

Title: 501 French Verbs
description: Barron's 501 French Verbs by Christopher Kendris is a comprehensive reference guide covering 501 French verbs fully conjugated in 14 tenses. It includes every verb needed for everyday conversational use, with proverbs, expressions, pronunciation hints, and drills at the back. Each verb is listed alphabetically with its conjugation in all tenses and moods. Part of a bestselling series for language learners, it is one of the most comprehensive collections of French verb tables available.

Title: Larousse French English Dictionary
description: An updated, easy-to-read dictionary of the French language designed for a wide range of interests and professions. Provides keys to pronunciation, idioms, and conjugations in both French and English.

Title: Collins-Robert French-English, English-French Dictionary
description: This edition draws on Collins' and Le Robert's multi-million word databases of contemporary English and French to ensure the user has a complete and accurate picture of the contemporary language. It includes boxes to help with the most typical translation problems and has highlighted many of the most common lexical structures in both languages. In two colours, it makes looking up words very easy, and the dictionary also covers grammar points such as genders, plurals, irregular verbs, etc.

Title: Detective LaRue: Letters from the Investigation
description: The canine detective Ike LaRue, an obedience school dropout, sets out to prove his innocence when he is wrongly accused of terrorizing the Hibbins' cats and stealing their cat treats. Told through humorous letters to his vacationing owner Mrs. LaRue, with split-screen illustrations showing two versions of the same events. When a string of canary burglaries stalls the police investigation, Ike flees custody and takes matters into his own paws.

Title: Collins Gem French Dictionary
description: A compact bilingual French-English dictionary in paperback mini format. With over 600 pages, it covers a wide range of subjects from basic vocabulary to more specialized terminology. Its compact size makes it easy to carry around as a portable reference.

Title: Dirty Hands
description: A seven-act political drama set in the fictional country of Illyria between 1943 and 1945. The story revolves around Hugo, a young man charged with assassinating the leader of the communist party who wants to adopt controversial policies. The play explores themes of political idealism versus Realpolitik, the morality of violence in politics, and whether one can govern innocently. The play was met with controversy, with left-wing populations boycotting performances while right-wing party members welcomed it.

Title: Merriam-Webster's French-English Dictionary
description: A bilingual, bidirectional guide to French and North American English with extensive coverage of Canadian French. Features more than 80,000 entries and 100,000 translations with abundant examples of words used in context. Special sections include French Grammar, Conjugation of French Verbs, Common French Abbreviations, and French Numbers.

Title: Larousse Pocket Dictionary: French-English/English-French
description: An ideal dictionary for everyday use for anyone speaking, reading, or studying French. With its handy portable paperback format and clear, easy-to-use layout, it includes 180,000 words, phrases, and translations, hundreds of usage examples, abbreviations, acronyms, proper nouns, and verb conjugation tables.

Title: Hedgie Blasts Off!
description: More than anything Hedgie wants to be an astronaut and travel into space. He gets his chance the day the geyser Big Sparkler starts to sputter and fade on the tiny planet of Mikkop. Because only Hedgie can fit into the small spaceship, he flies to Mikkop where he finds out what is clogging up Big Sparkler and fixes the problem in an unusual way. He returns home a hero amid cheers from the aliens and scientists. Features an amazing gatefold illustration.

Title: Honey... Honey... Lion! A Story from Africa
description: For as long as anyone can remember, the honeyguide bird and the African honey badger have been partners when it comes to honey. Honeyguide finds the honeycomb, Badger breaks it open, and they share the sweetness inside. But this day, Badger keeps the honey for himself. The sly bird teaches him a lesson by leading him to a tree that camouflages not a hive but a ferocious lion. A humorous yet important moral about the importance of expressing appreciation. Ages 4-8.

Title: I Wanna Iguana
description: Alex just has to convince his mom to let him have an iguana, so he puts his arguments in writing. He promises that she won't have to feed it or clean its cage or even see it if she doesn't want to. Of course Mom imagines life with a six-foot-long iguana eating them out of house and home. The back-and-forth correspondence through notes will have kids in hysterics. The lively, imaginative illustrations show their polar opposite dreams of life with an iguana.

Title: Froggy Eats Out
description: Froggy is going out to dinner with his parents for their anniversary. He's hoping for burgers and flies, but Froggy's mother tells him Chez Yum is a very fancy restaurant. Froggy puts on his best clothes. At the restaurant, his mother warns him to 'be neat, be quiet, and don't put your feet on the table.' It's just too much to ask of a lively little frog. Froggy can't help himself as he sucks on ice and sugar, slurps spaghetti and flies, and flops and sings.

Title: Fireflies
description: A young boy is proud of having caught a jar full of fireflies, which seems to him like owning a piece of moonlight. But as the light begins to dim, he realizes he must set the insects free, or they will die. A timely reminiscence of a happy childhood memory that will linger in the minds of its readers.

Title: Miss Smith's Incredible Storybook
description: When Zack meets his second-grade teacher, Miss Smith, he can tell right away that her class will be different. Miss Smith has a knack for telling tales: when she reads from her incredible book, the stories literally spring to life. Then one day Miss Smith is late for school. The principal takes over and things get out of hand. The classroom is swarming with storybook characters from princesses to pirates to the three little pigs.

Title: Froggy Plays T-Ball
description: It's the first day of T-ball and Froggy can't wait. In his dreams he's hitting home runs and waving to the cheering crowd. But things never go quite right for Froggy. After he bonks his pal Max on the head with his bat, throws himself out at first base, and spends his time in the outfield catching real flies with his tongue, Froggy has one last chance to prove he's a T-ball star.

Title: The Big Field
description: Playing shortstop is a way of life for Hutch, not only is his hero Derek Jeter a shortstop, but so was his father, a former local legend turned pro. Which is why having to play second base feels like demotion. Yet that's where Hutch ends up after Darryl 'D-Will' Williams, the best shortstop prospect since A-Rod, joins the team. Hutch is cool with playing in D-Will's shadow, until the two shortstops in Hutch's life betray him in a way he never could have imagined.

Title: Back-to-School Fright from the Black Lagoon (Black Lagoon Adventures, No. 13)
description: The Black Lagoon Adventures series follows Hubie as he faces comically exaggerated fears about everyday school situations. In this installment, Hubie faces his back-to-school anxieties with humor. (AI)

Title: The Seer of Shadows
description: The time is 1872. The place is New York City. Horace Carpetine has been raised to believe in science and rationality. So as apprentice to Enoch Middleditch, a society photographer, he thinks of his trade as a scientific art. But when wealthy society matron Mrs. Frederick Von Macht orders a photographic portrait, strange things begin to happen. Horace's first real photographs reveal a frightful likeness: it's the image of the Von Machts' dead daughter, Eleanora.

Title: The Little Snowflake
description: In this whimsical adventure, a bold snowflake leaps from his cloud in hopes of being part of a big snowman.

Title: Go, Dog. Go!
description: Written for beginning readers using only 75 different words, this beloved Beginner Book by P.D. Eastman features all kinds of wonderful dogs riding bicycles, scooters, skis, roller skates, and driving all sorts of vehicles on their way to a party held on top of a tree. Using a 75-word vocabulary and clear illustrations, pre-readers are introduced to the concepts of number, colour, and opposites.

Title: Curious George
description: Curious George tells the story of how George came to America with the Man in the Yellow Hat. Taken from his home in the jungles of Africa, George's inquisitive nature initiates his many adventures including sailing the seas, accidentally calling in the fire department, and flying high above the city. The first Curious George story was published in 1941 and has become a treasured classic.

Title: If You Give a Cat a Cupcake
description: When you give a cat a cupcake, he'll ask for some sprinkles to go with it. When you give him the sprinkles, he might spill some on the floor. Cleaning up will make him hot, so you'll give him a bathing suit... and that's just the beginning! The lovable cat who first appeared in If You Give a Pig a Party now stars in his very own book in the bestselling If You Give... series.

Title: Night of the New Magicians
description: Merlin sends Jack and Annie on a mysterious mission to Paris, France, over 100 years ago. There they must find four magicians and give them an urgent message from Merlin himself. When Jack and Annie land in Paris, they make their way to the 1889 World's Fair. Below the Eiffel Tower, built especially for the fair, there are thousands of exhibits from all over the world. But how will they find the magicians in the crowds? Formerly Magic Tree House #35.

Title: Itchy, Itchy Chicken Pox
description: Bouncy rhymes make this lively story about coping with chicken pox one that children will love to read long after their itches are gone. A boy who had chicken pox has to take an oatmeal bath, feels itchy, and has spots everywhere. His daddy counts his spots. When he was better, he got to go to school. Full color. A rhyming Level 1 Scholastic Reader.

Title: More Spaghetti, I Say!
description: A hilarious story about a monkey who loves spaghetti, perfect for beginner readers! Minnie the monkey loves spaghetti so much that she's too busy eating it to even play. Can she convince her friend that spaghetti is the best? With hilarious, easy-to-read text and colorful artwork, little ones are sure to giggle along with this Level 1 reader. Seuss-like in rhyme and silliness.

Title: Julius, the Baby of the World
description: Lilly, a mouse of decided feelings, feels that her new brother Julius leaves much to be desired. Before baby Julius is born, Lilly is an exemplary sister, but once he arrives she has a hard time controlling her jealousy. Her parents dote on the new infant and coo 'Julius is the baby of the world,' but Lilly thinks he is disgusting. When cousin Garland criticizes Julius, Lilly springs to his defense and has a change of heart. Ages 4-up.

Title: 501 Spanish Verbs
description: Barron's 501 Spanish Verbs by Christopher Kendris is a comprehensive reference guide covering 501 Spanish verbs fully conjugated across all tenses and moods. The book provides a step-by-step guide to conjugating regular and irregular verbs, with exercises and quizzes to help learners practice. Includes an English-Spanish index, examples of verbs used in context, and audio resources for pronunciation. An invaluable resource for students, travelers, and anyone seeking proficiency in Spanish.

Title: The Berenstain Bears' New Baby
description: This brightly illustrated story tells of a youngster's happy acceptance of a new addition to the family and how Mama and Papa Bear assist him with the adjustment. Just after the Bear family moves into their new treehouse, Mama, Papa, and Small Bear make room for the new baby. This book is notable for introducing Sister Bear to the series. Learning to deal with change!

Title: Hello Reader, Level 1: Footprints in the Snow
description: A perfect book for intrepid young naturalists, this Level 1 Hello Reader introduces young children to the tracks that different animals leave in the snow as they hurry to their homes. Children first see the tracks and then follow the footprints into the homes of animals such as a deer, an owl, and a bear, as well as a little girl. Full color.

Title: Wheelock's Latin
description: For nearly sixty years, Wheelock's Latin has remained the optimus liber of beginning Latin textbooks. When Professor Frederic M. Wheelock's Latin first appeared in 1956, the reviews extolled its thoroughness, organization, and conciseness. Now, nearly six decades later, that prediction has certainly proved accurate. The book features 40 chapters with grammatical explanations and readings drawn from the works of Rome's major prose and verse writers, self-tutorial exercises with answer keys, an extensive English-Latin/Latin-English vocabulary section, and a rich selection of original Latin readings.

Title: The New College Latin & English Dictionary
description: A comprehensive Latin-English and English-Latin dictionary by John Traupman. Designed for students and scholars, it provides extensive vocabulary coverage with clear translations and grammatical information. An authoritative reference for Latin language learners. (AI)

Title: The Night Before First Grade
description: It's the night before the Big Day - first grade. Penny is excited to start the year with her best friend right beside her in the same classroom. This humorous take on Clement C. Moore's classic tale has a perfect twist ending that will surprise readers - as well as the heroine of the story - and help all about-to-be first-graders through their own back-to-school jitters.

Title: The Sandcastle Contest
description: When Matthew enters a contest at the beach, he builds an amazing, incredible, unbelievable sandcastle! Matthew's family is off to the cottage. When they arrive, there is a sandcastle contest going on. Matthew builds a magnificent sand house - one so good that the judges can't even tell it's not real! With classic Munsch wit and Martchenko style, The Sandcastle Contest is a fun-filled story perfect to read at home or on the beach!

Title: My Teacher for President
description: Oliver has been learning about the president's job, and his teacher would be the perfect candidate. She loves white houses, she's used to being followed everywhere, she attends lots of meetings, she finds jobs for people, and she believes in peace. Images of Oliver's teacher depicted during a typical school day are contrasted with scenes of his heroine carrying out presidential duties.

Title: Alvin Ho: Allergic to Girls, School, and Other Scary Things
description: The first book in a hilarious chapter book series that tackles anxiety in a fun, kid-friendly way. Alvin, an Asian American second grader, is afraid of everything - elevators, tunnels, girls, and, most of all, school. He's so afraid of school that, while he's there, he never, ever, says a word. But at home, Alvin is a very loud superhero named Firecracker Man, a brother to Calvin and Anibelly, and a gentleman-in-training, just like his dad.

Title: Boundless Grace
description: In the picture book Amazing Grace, children were introduced to a spunky little African American girl named Grace. In this sequel, she travels to Gambia, West Africa to visit her father and his new family. This is a simply told story with the subtle message that parents should maintain family bonds despite divorce. A trip to Banjul, Gambia helps Grace come to terms with her father and understand that there are many types of families.

Title: Winnie Ille Pu
description: The publishing history of Winnie Ille Pu is among the most famous in all of publishing annals: how a privately printed Latin translation of A.A. Milne's Winnie-the-Pooh, originally issued in a 300-copy edition, eventually became the only book in Latin ever to grace the New York Times bestseller list. Whether you're calling on long-ago high-school Latin lessons or are fully proficient in the language, you'll delight in once again meeting Christopher Robin, Eeyore, Owl, Piglet, tiny Roo, and, of course, Pooh himself.

Title: Best Teacher Ever
description: A children's book by Mercer Mayer about a student who thinks his teacher is the best. Features Mayer's signature Little Critter characters as they celebrate the special bond between teachers and students. (AI)

Title: Alphabet Rescue
description: While on vacation in Alphabet City, 'Little e' and the other lowercase letters repair an old fire truck and come to the rescue when a fire engulfs the letter-making factory. All the little letters from Charley's alphabet are disappointed when the capital letters won't let them have fun with the grown-up fire truck. Join the action as those smart little letters build a fire truck of their very own and come to the rescue just in time!

Title: Basics of Biblical Greek Grammar
description: The standard textbook for beginning Greek language students in colleges and seminaries. William D. Mounce offers a clear, understandable, integrated approach to learning New Testament Greek. The fourth edition is updated throughout based on feedback from professors and students. Features 40 chapters with grammatical explanations, readings from the New Testament, self-tutorial exercises with answer keys, and extensive vocabulary sections.

Title: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature
description: Walter Bauer's comprehensive Greek-English lexicon, commonly known as BDAG (Bauer-Danker-Arndt-Gingrich). This reference work is indispensable for serious New Testament Greek study, featuring over 200,000 cross-references and thousands of new entries to supplement the legacy of previous editions.

Title: Greek Grammar Beyond the Basics: An Exegetical Syntax of the New Testament
description: Daniel B. Wallace's intermediate Greek grammar for second-year students. Provides concise, up-to-date guidance for intermediate Greek students to do accurate exegesis of biblical texts. Features semantic categories with definitions and keys to identification, plus scores of charts and tables.

Title: Basics of Biblical Greek Workbook
description: A companion workbook to William Mounce's textbook, designed for use in beginning Greek courses. Helps reinforce learning through a variety of exercises.

Title: Lexical Aids for Students of New Testament Greek
description: Bruce M. Metzger's venerable resource organizes Greek words according to their frequency of appearance in the New Testament. Divided into two parts: words listed by frequency, and words grouped according to their root. Includes appendixes for learning Greek prepositions and principal parts of verbs.

Title: Vine's Expository Dictionary of New Testament Words
description: W. E. Vine's classic expository dictionary provides an exhaustive presentation of synonymous Greek words in the New Testament. Comments are given on various passages, doctrines of Scripture are dealt with at length, and notes are provided on matters historical, technical, and etymological. Partakes to a considerable extent of the nature of a concordance.

Title: New Testament Greek for Beginners
description: J. Gresham Machen's standard text for learning Greek, first published in 1923. Intended for students beginning the study of the Greek Testament without previous acquaintance with the Greek language. An instruction book, not a descriptive grammar, with everything made subservient to imparting a reading acquaintance with the language.

Title: The Complete Vocabulary Guide to the Greek New Testament
description: Warren C. Trenchard's comprehensive guide sets a new standard as the most complete book of its kind. Covers the entire vocabulary of the Greek New Testament, including Goodrick/Kohlenberger numbers for cross-referencing, principal parts of all verbs used in the New Testament, and a frequency list of words.

Title: Essentials of New Testament Greek
description: Ray Summers' introductory textbook for learning New Testament Greek. Covers the basics of Greek grammar and vocabulary for beginners.

Title: Theological Dictionary of the New Testament
description: Gerhard Kittel's monumental 10-volume work treats more than 2,000 theologically significant Greek words. Each entry explains a word's secular background, its role in the Old Testament and extrabiblical literature, and its various uses in the New Testament. A necessity for serious students of Greek.
